content.problemStatement:
Hasan is preparing a party for his MMM friends (numbered 111 through MMM). He bought NNN baskets of candies (numbered 111 through NNN); for each valid iii, the iii-th basket contains aia_iai​ candies.

Each friend should get at most one candy. For each valid iii, the iii-th friend would like to get a candy either from one of the first LiL_iLi​ baskets or one of the last RiR_iRi​ baskets, i.e. a from basket bbb (1≤b≤N1 \le b \le N1≤b≤N) such that b≤Lib \le L_ib≤Li​ or b≥N+1−Rib \ge N+1-R_ib≥N+1−Ri​.

Hasan loves his friends, so he wants as many of them as possible to get the candies they want. If he distributes the candies optimally, what is the maximum number of his friends who will get the candies they want?
constraints: 0≤Li,Ri≤N0 \le L_i, R_i \le N0≤Li​,Ri​≤N for each valid iii
0≤Li+Ri≤N0 \le L_i + R_i \le N0≤Li​+Ri​≤N for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​.
MMM lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤M1 \le i \le M1≤i≤M), the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum number of Hasan's friends who can get the candies they want.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We can give a candy from basket 111 to the 222-nd friend, a candy from basket 222 to the 111-st friend and a candy from basket 333 to the 333-rd friend.
